message:
feat(workflows): add ConcurrencyLimitReached variant and active lock count

When the number of concurrently running workflows hits the configured
maximum, the runner needs a distinct signal to distinguish "pane is
locked by another workflow" from "global concurrency budget exhausted."
Previously there was no way to represent this, so the runner would
either silently skip or report a misleading PaneLocked error.

Changes:
- runner.rs: Add WorkflowStartResult::ConcurrencyLimitReached { active,
  limit } variant with is_started()=false, is_locked()=false,
  execution_id()=None semantics. Handle it in the detection loop with
  a debug-level log
- lock.rs: Add active_count() to PaneWorkflowLockManager returning the
  number of currently held locks, enabling the runner to check against
  the configured limit before attempting to start
- mod.rs: Include the new variant in the exhaustive test arrays for
  variant equality and predicate coverage

Tests:
- start_result_concurrency_limit_reached: predicate assertions
- start_result_serde_concurrency_limit_reached: JSON roundtrip

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
